commit.message:
feat: DAG critical path wave parallelism + evidence envelope standardization (#97)

* feat: DAG critical path wave parallelism + evidence envelope standardization

Task 1 — dag_scheduler.ts / worker_batch_planner.ts:
- Add computeCriticalPathLength(graph): returns max wave number (critical path depth)
- Add computeWaveParallelismBound(total, depth, opts): ceil(total/depth) clamped [1,8]
- Update microBatch() concurrency resolution: explicit maxConcurrent > criticalPathLength
  > static default 3; backward-compatible (no opts → same default-3 behaviour)
- Enforce wave boundaries in buildRoleExecutionBatches: plans from different waves
  are never co-batched into the same worker context window; each wave slice is
  independently packed and carries a wave field for observability

Task 2 — evidence_envelope.ts / evolution_executor.ts / athena_reviewer.ts:
- Introduce src/core/evidence_envelope.ts: canonical shared types (VerificationEvidence,
  EvidenceEnvelope, PrChecksSnapshot) in a standalone module to prevent the circular
  import that would arise from executor ↔ reviewer cross-importing
- Export buildVerificationEvidence() from evolution_executor.ts: maps raw command
  results to {build,tests,lint} slots with sticky-fail semantics
- Populate verificationEvidence in athenaInput — enables Athena's deterministic
  fast-path (skips AI call for clean passes where build+tests=pass)
- Type runAthenaPostmortem's workerResult as EvidenceEnvelope for contract clarity

Tests: +19 (2888 total, 0 fail)
- computeCriticalPathLength / computeWaveParallelismBound: 5 new tests
- microBatch with criticalPathLength + precedence: 2 new tests
- buildVerificationEvidence slot mapping + sticky-fail + negative path: 9 new tests
- wave-boundary enforcement in buildRoleExecutionBatches: 3 new tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: auto-resolve SSH identity key for direct connections (#906)

* fix: auto-resolve SSH identity key for direct connections

azlin connect, ssh_exec, and log follow all use subprocess SSH but
never passed -i ~/.ssh/azlin_key when --key wasn't explicitly provided.
This caused 'Permission denied (publickey)' on hosts where the only
key is ~/.ssh/azlin_key (no id_rsa/id_ed25519).

Fix: use resolve_ssh_key() (prefers azlin_key → id_rsa) and inject
-i <key> into SSH args at all three call sites:
- cmd_connect.rs: interactive connect
- main.rs: ssh_exec background commands
- cmd_sync_ops.rs: log follow streaming

The bastion path already called resolve_ssh_key(); the native russh
path already resolved it via native_ssh::resolve_key_path(). Only
the subprocess SSH paths were missing it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(analytics): emit dual analytics channels (#96)

* feat: convert architecture drift output into actionable debt tasks in planning flow

Add buildDriftDebtTasks() to prometheus.ts that deterministically converts
ArchitectureDriftReport items (stale file references + deprecated token usages)
into structured debt plan tasks injected into parsed.plans.

Previously, drift data was only surfaced as a prompt hint and a confidence
penalty — AI output could silently omit remediation tasks. Now drift items are
always tracked in the plan graph regardless of AI output.

Key design choices:
- Groups items by source doc (one task per doc per issue type) to avoid task explosion
- Places debt tasks on wave maxWave+1 so they don't block critical delivery work
- Suppresses generation when an existing AI plan already names the doc
- Marks tasks with taskKind='debt' and source='architecture_drift' for filtering
- Sets _driftDebtTaskCount on parsed output for observability
- Filters null/undefined entries in drift ref arrays defensively

Tests added in prometheus_parse.test.ts covering:
- Empty/null input guards
- Task grouping per doc for stale refs and deprecated tokens
- Wave placement relative to existing plans
- Duplicate suppression when AI already covers a doc
- Required packet fields on generated tasks
- Negative path: malformed entries don't throw

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(analytics): emit dual analytics channels to separate metric semantics from degradation

Add cycle_health.json as a dedicated degradation channel alongside the
existing cycle_analytics.json (performance/semantic channel).

Problem: cycle_analytics.json previously conflated KPI timing values
(which change when metric definitions change) with threshold-relative
health signals (which should only change on genuine runtime degradation).
Consumers could not distinguish a semantic metric change from a real
regression.

Solution: two separate files, two separate concerns.

  cycle_analytics.json  — performance/semantic channel (unchanged)
    KPI latencies, funnel counts/rates, outcomes, confidence, causal links.
    Values here may change when metric definitions evolve.

  cycle_health.json     — degradation channel (new)
    SLO breach status/count, causal-link anomaly flags, derived healthScore
    (healthy | degraded | critical).  This file changes ONLY when the system
    genuinely degrades relative to configured thresholds.

New exports from cycle_analytics.ts:
  - computeCycleHealth(analyticsRecord) — pure fn, no file I/O
  - persistCycleHealth(config, healthRecord) — rolling history, same cap
  - readCycleHealth(config) — reads cycle_health.json or null
  - CYCLE_HEALTH_SCHEMA — canonical schema with healthScoreEnum
  - HEALTH_SCORE — enum: healthy | degraded | critical

Orchestrator: persistCycleHealth is called immediately after
persistCycleAnalytics in the same advisory try/catch block, so analytics
failure still never blocks orchestration.

Tests: 30 new assertions covering schema, health-score derivation,
persistence, retention policy, null-input safety, and the dual-channel
separation invariant (health file must not contain raw KPI fields).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
